headline: OpenAI launches first chip via Broadcom deal
detail: OpenAI unveiled its first in-house chip developed with Broadcom as part of a push to own more of the AI stack. This is directly bullish for AVGO, your largest (and currently losing) position — it validates Broadcom's AI custom chip business. Watch for a bounce; it could be the relief catalyst AVGO needs.

headline: Qualcomm acquires AI startup Modular for data center push
detail: Qualcomm is buying AI software startup Modular to strengthen its data center and software stack ambitions. This signals continued big-money conviction in AI infrastructure buildout, which is broadly supportive of your chip and AI names like AVGO, SMH, and CRWD's ecosystem. Not a direct mover today but confirms the sector thesis.

headline: Strait of Hormuz tension eases on Trump-Iran signals
detail: Trump says Iran assured the U.S. there won't be tolls on the Strait of Hormuz, and the Treasury will oversee released Iranian funds. Lower geopolitical risk in the region reduces oil price spike fears, which is a mild macro positive — keeps risk appetite alive for tech and growth names in your book.
